resources.string: To use the app, enable accessibility features
For proper operation, grant the app \"notification access\"
The car will assume that music is being played from this source
Removes the system label \"Unknown Artist\" when the music author is not specified
You have "ES File Explorer" installed. This program may not work correctly with it. It is recommended to uninstall it before use. Instead, you can conveniently use "File Manager+"

Tap here to grant the app additional file management permissions. This is necessary because the system may block access to the cover art cache in the public directory. As a result, the app won\'t be able to clear files and free up disk space on its own.
Update all media data with each synchronization, including those that may not have changed since the last update
If disabled, sends tap events only to MacroDroid
Triggers a long press event after a specified time. Works for buttons that send KeyEvent. If a button does not send KeyEvent, the standard long press handler will be used
After a single short tap, a brief pause follows to wait for a second tap. If a second tap occurs within the time window, a double tap event is triggered
Prevents triggering a parallel short press during custom long presses.
If other buttons are held during a long press event, a multi-long event is triggered instead of a regular long press
Blocks sending intents, only logs. For testing key presses without triggering actual button actions
Enable this feature to use steering wheel buttons for track switching, pausing, and playing. When another media app is opened, control automatically switches to it.
Select apps that should take over media control when they appear on the screen
Advanced control together with native audio sources
Automatically disable media control when the system climate settings window appears on the screen.
Hide media widget on home screen
Hides the widget on the home screen using system API capabilities. Allows swiping left on the home screen to access the standard app menu
Sends intents with information about the currently playing track and playback state
Show detailed logs of initialization and state changes
Are you sure you want to delete the key binding?
Press the button or combination of buttons in the way you want to bind them to the action
Select the action you want to perform when triggered
Will launch the selected application when triggered
When triggered, will launch applications from the list in sequence
Switch between the last active media app and the last navigator
Choose your primary navigator to open
No supported navigator app is installed
Choose the applications you want to launch
Drag items to set the order in which carousel apps are launched
Select players for which playback should start automatically after opening.
When triggered, it will launch the selected shortcut
Select a shortcut from another app that will be launched when the bind is triggered
This shortcut will be launched when the bind is triggered
Do you want to import this data?
Automatically disable media control during incoming or outgoing calls.

Everything you do in this section is at your own risk. Indiscriminate changes to parameters and values may cause harm to your vehicle. There are commands here that can put your head unit into Recovery mode. Be cautious and think carefully before making changes. May God help you.
Functions added to \"Favorites\" will be displayed here
Drag up the modes you want to switch between. The order is also taken into account.
Are you sure you want to block button actions?
Playback from paired phones and other Bluetooth devices
iPhone link; Apple CarPlay interface on the head unit
Other Android and system music players (media session and audio focus)
Drag up the audio sources you want to switch between in order.
The last used driving mode will be restored after a system restart
Select which mode should be restored after the vehicle starts
The driving mode selected at the time the vehicle is turned off is saved and automatically restored on the next start.
A short notification sound in the headrest when changing the driving mode
Select the driving mode for which a notification sound should be played upon switching
When activated, sets the selected driving mode. Activating again returns to the previous mode.
Sequentially switches between the selected driving modes.
Drag up the modes you want to switch sequentially
Saves a full backup of settings, excluding selected media apps
Do you want to import a full backup of settings? Your current settings will be lost. You will need to confirm the required permissions before applying.
Display selected driving mode on screen
Visualize the selected driving mode on the tablet, including custom modes
Balanced settings for everyday driving: standard traction and comfortable steering.
Smooth traction and light steering for relaxed trips and maximum comfort.
Fuel/energy efficiency: smooth throttle response and early gear shifts.
Sporty response: sharper throttle, longer gear holds, and heavier steering.
Enhanced sport calibration: maximum response speed and holding lower gears (availability depends on the model).
Maximum performance: powertrain delivers peak traction (in hybrids — combined operation of ICE and electric motor).
Adaptive mode: automatically selects eco/comfort/sport profile based on driving style and road; in hybrids, it decides when to engage the ICE.
Custom profile: save your preferences for traction, steering, and suspension (if available).
Pure electric mode: ICE activates only under high load or low battery.
Default hybrid: system automatically balances ICE and electric motor for traction and efficiency.
Battery preservation: maintains battery level at a set value (PHEV "hold" mode).
Eco for hybrids: prioritizes electric drive, smooth response, and moderate climate power for minimal consumption.
Plug-in hybrid mode: focuses on hybrid logic (battery maintenance/discharge); on some models, identical to "Hybrid".
Permanent all-wheel drive/clutch preload for stability and traction on slippery surfaces.
Electric AWD: engages rear axle with electric motor for instant traction and stabilization.
Off-road: reconfigures traction, transmission, and stabilization systems for uneven terrain.
Snow/ice: soft throttle, gentle starts, and enhanced traction control.
Sand: maintains speed and allows wheel slip to avoid getting stuck.
Mud: more low-end traction and permissible slip to maintain momentum.
Rocks: ultra-smooth traction delivery at low speeds for crawling.
Hill Descent Control: maintains low speed on steep descents by braking individual wheels.
Service start preset (calibration/diagnostics); not a user mode.
Internal designation (no public description); likely off-road/long-distance calibration.

Even if a function has a negative status, it may remain operational and supported. Similarly, a positive status does not always guarantee the function\'s correct operation. The status reflects how the system classifies the current function. The actual functionality must be verified experimentally.
The use of non-standard driving modes and their impact on vehicle components and assemblies is not sufficiently studied. Use them at your own risk.
If you are already using mode restoration via DebugM or MConfig, it’s better to stick to one. GIB may restore modes slightly faster, which can disrupt the mode restoration logic in DebugM.
Here you can leave useful information about the function: what values it accepts, its features, what works correctly, and what doesn’t, etc.
Failed to retrieve data. Please check your internet connection.
Notes are visible to everyone, and anyone can edit them. Please be kind.
Listeners do not work for all sensors; functionality is determined experimentally.
Enter the phone number to be called
Select a file to import all settings. Your current settings will be lost.
To free up a button action for binding via GIB:
Enabling and disabling 360-degree surround view cameras
Opens CarPlay if hidden, hides it if open. Works when CarPlay is connected.
Choose which CarPlay screen to open
Main CarPlay screen, same as default launch
Sequential switching of selected exterior lighting modes.

To free up a button action for custom bindings, select one of the green (Custom) actions. After this, the button action will become available in GIB. For Atlas, additional steps are required to disable.
Opens the built-in app launcher. Repeated activation closes the launcher.
The higher the quality of icon images, the more memory they require and the slower they load.
Padding from window edges to icons
There is nothing here yet. Press the "Add" button on the top panel.\n\nThe "Lock" button enables or disables the free movement mode, in which you can drag and arrange icons in the desired order.
This is a preview for customizing the icon appearance
Hidden system settings menu where you can enable or disable system cleanup (on Preface, downloaded maps and music will be preserved).
This is a header displayed among launcher icons that logically separates subgroups of apps, activities, and shortcuts.

After opening this system dialog in the standard settings (black with a car), an additional non-localized button may appear. It can only be removed by resetting to factory settings. Also, if you disable automatic cleanup, ensure that the device\'s memory does not become full, otherwise the tablet may fail to boot.
Enter a new title to be displayed in the \"My apps\" tab
Are you sure you want to freeze this app? It will no longer be available for launch.
Warning, this is a system app. Disabling it may cause the system to work incorrectly! Are you sure you want to freeze this app? It will no longer be available for launch.
Your dividers currently look like this
Preview of the test section title
Select the settings sections you want to import. You will need to confirm the required permissions before applying.
Select the settings sections to export. During import, you can specify which ones to restore.
A section displaying the history of previously launched apps will appear at the bottom of the launcher. This feature requires the accessibility service to be enabled.
Show apps that cannot be launched in the all-apps grid.
Do you want to unfreeze and launch
Enable only if you know what you are doing. This is a safeguard against dangerous actions inside the system.
It seems the %s app is not installed
Opens additional capabilities in the launcher: detects application status in the system (running/not running), force-stop, and other functions.
Stop DIM interaction on system boot
Automatically stops the DIM interaction service (which blocks login and interferes with some apps) every time the system starts.
Disabling this feature prevents the rear wiper from automatically activating when reverse gear (R) is engaged.
Combines Windows-style Alt+Tab menu with Android Recents. Multiple activations cycle through apps. You can manually close running processes by swiping up or tapping the cross.
ADB must be enabled for this feature to work
If long presses on the Menu button are not detected, try enabling the alternative press detection method for this button
If long presses on the Mute button are not detected, try enabling the alternative press detection method for this button
Expands the previously opened application (if it was not closed)
Controls all sources: Android, radio, and Bluetooth. Everything is handled through the app
Controls only the active Android media session. Radio and Bluetooth are handled by the firmware
Choose apps where media control should be automatically disabled while they are in the foreground
Manage applications in the default system launcher. Select which apps to show and their display order.
GAppStorage is required. Please install it first.

1) The order of apps in the launcher cannot be set because the stock launcher disrupts the sequence using HashMap before displaying them.\n2) After removing all apps from the launcher, you need to reboot the head unit for the changes to take effect.

The stock launcher is not designed to be used in the same way as on regular devices. When updating apps displayed in the launcher (and in some other cases), it may work incorrectly — some apps may disappear from the list. The issue is usually resolved by restarting the launcher or rebooting the device. Are you sure you want to restart the stock launcher?

Launcher Control

Show/hide launcher:
In MacroDroid: Actions -> Send Intent
Target: Broadcast
Action: com.salat.gbinder.TOGGLE_LAUNCHER
Package: com.salat.gbinder

Receiving Key Press Events
In MacroDroid: Triggers -> Intent Received

Actions:
\"com.salat.gbinder.SHORT_CLICK\"
\"com.salat.gbinder.LONG_PRESS\"
\"com.salat.gbinder.MULTI_LONG_PRESS\"
\"com.salat.gbinder.DOUBLE_CLICK\"
\"com.salat.gbinder.HOLD_START\"
\"com.salat.gbinder.HOLD_STOP\"
\"com.salat.gbinder.KEY_EVENT\"

Additional:
\"code\" -> keyCode of the key.
for KEY_EVENT additionally: \"event\" -> [0|1|3] where 0 = DOWN, 1 = UP, 3 = CANCEL
and \"func\" -> system function code

for MULTI_LONG_PRESS, the \"code\" parameter is received in the format -> 119+200087 with key codes, the code is formed from the smaller number to the larger

Working with Audio Sources

To listen for changes in audio sources on the head unit:
In MacroDroid: Triggers -> Intent Received
Action: com.salat.gbinder.AUDIO_SOURCE_CHANGED
Additional: source=[USB|BT|RADIO|CPAA|ONLINE|OTHER|YUNTING|UNKNOWN]

To change the current audio source of the head unit:
In MacroDroid: Actions -> Send Intent
Target: Broadcast
Action: com.salat.gbinder.SET_AUDIO_SOURCE
Package: com.salat.gbinder
Additional 1: target=[USB|BT|RADIO|CPAA|ONLINE|OTHER|YUNTING|UNKNOWN]
Additional 2: optional source=[UNKNOWN|WECARFLOW|KUWO|KUGOU|NETEASE_CLOUD|SOHU|JINRI|QYS|OTHER|FANDENG|TENCENT_VIDEO|XMLY|GC|HUOSHAN|BILIBILI|THUNDER_VOICE|CMVIDEO_VOICE]
Additional 3: optional autoplay=[1|0]

Media Control

Enabling and disabling media control:
In MacroDroid: Actions -> Send Intent
Target: Broadcast

Action: com.salat.gbinder.TEMPORARY_DISABLE_MEDIA_CONTROL
Additional: duration -> Duration of disabling in seconds

Other actions:
com.salat.gbinder.ENABLE_MEDIA_CONTROL
com.salat.gbinder.SMART_ENABLE_MEDIA_CONTROL
com.salat.gbinder.DISABLE_MEDIA_CONTROL

Package: com.salat.gbinder
"SMART" - Enables only if it was previously enabled manually

Receiving Active Media Session Data

Playback status information:
Action: com.salat.gbinder.PLAYBACK_STATE
Additional: isPlaying=[0|1] -> 1 playback is active, 0 nothing is playing

Current track data:
Action: com.salat.gbinder.PLAYBACK_METADATA
Additional 1: id -> unique track identifier
Additional 2: packageName -> package name of the app playing the media session
Additional 3: appName -> application name
Additional 4: title -> title
Additional 5: artist -> artist
Additional 6: album -> album name
Additional 7: uri -> track URI
Additional 8: coverUri -> cover art URI

Intents are sent only when media data changes; you can manually request them via an outgoing broadcast:
Action: com.salat.gbinder.REQUEST_PLAYBACK_INFO

Working with Phone Calls

In MacroDroid: Actions -> Send Intent

Make a phone call
Action 1: com.salat.gbinder.PHONE_CALL
Additional: number -> phone number (specify the variable type as "string", otherwise the "+" in the number will be lost)

Answer a call
Action 2: com.salat.gbinder.ANSWER_CALL

Reject a call
Action 3: com.salat.gbinder.REJECT_CALL

Disconnect a call
Action 5: com.salat.gbinder.DISCONNECT_CALL

Camera Control

Show/hide 360 cameras:
In MacroDroid: Actions -> Send Intent
Target: Broadcast
Actions:
com.salat.gbinder.TOGGLE_CAMERA
Package: com.salat.gbinder

Setting Configurator Value

In MacroDroid: Actions -> Send Intent
Target: Broadcast
Action1: com.salat.gbinder.SET_INT_PROPERTY
Action2: com.salat.gbinder.SET_FLOAT_PROPERTY
Package: com.salat.gbinder

Additional1: id -> parameter identifier
Additional2: value -> new value
Additional3: area -> scope of application (optional)

Retrieving Values and Subscribing to Value Changes

In MacroDroid: Actions -> Send Intent
Target: Broadcast
Action1: com.salat.gbinder.GET_INT_PROPERTY
Action2: com.salat.gbinder.GET_FLOAT_PROPERTY
Action3: com.salat.gbinder.LISTEN_PROPERTY_CHANGES
- subscribe once, and then an action will be received for each change of the specified function Package: com.salat.gbinder

Additional1: id -> parameter identifier
Additional2: area -> scope of application (optional)

In response to GET_*, the action com.salat.gbinder.PROPERTY_VALUE_RESULT is received
In response to LISTEN_*, the action com.salat.gbinder.PROPERTY_VALUE_CHANGED is received
Additional1: id = function identifier
Additional2: area = scope of application
Additional3: value = current value

Retrieving Sensor Values and Subscribing to Sensor Value Changes

In MacroDroid: Actions -> Send Intent
Target: Broadcast
Package: com.salat.gbinder

Action1: com.salat.gbinder.GET_INT_SENSOR
Action2: com.salat.gbinder.GET_FLOAT_SENSOR
Action3: com.salat.gbinder.LISTEN_SENSOR_CHANGES
- subscribe once, and then an action will be received for each sensor change. Not all sensors are supported; functionality is determined experimentally.
Additional: id -> parameter identifier

In response to GET_*_SENSOR, the action com.salat.gbinder.SENSOR_*_RESULT is received
In response to LISTEN_SENSOR_*, the action com.salat.gbinder.SENSOR_VALUE_CHANGED (for FLOAT values)
and com.salat.gbinder.SENSOR_*_CHANGED is received
Additional1: id = sensor identifier
Additional2: value = current value

Retrieving Info Parameter Values

In MacroDroid: Actions -> Send Intent
Target: Broadcast
Package: com.salat.gbinder

Action1: com.salat.gbinder.GET_INT_INFO
Action2: com.salat.gbinder.GET_FLOAT_INFO
Action3: com.salat.gbinder.GET_STRING_INFO

In response to GET_*_INFO, the action com.salat.gbinder.INFO_VALUE_RESULT is received
Additional1: id = parameter identifier
Additional2: value = current value
Select the applications that should be shown in the stock launcher grid.
Launcher applications have been updated successfully
